root_text.snp3.3:1.2: eka samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.snp3.3:2.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgatā vācā subhāsitā hoti, na dubbhāsitā, anavajjā ca ananuvajjā ca viññūnaṁ.
root_text.snp3.3:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu subhāsitaṁyeva bhāsati no dubbhāsitaṁ, dhammaṁyeva bhāsati no adhammaṁ, piyaṁyeva bhāsati no appiyaṁ, saccaṁyeva bhāsati no alikaṁ.
root_text.snp3.3:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi aṅgehi samannāgatā vācā subhāsitā hoti, no dubbhāsitā, anavajjā ca ananuvajjā ca viññūnan”ti.
root_text.snp3.3:2.6: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato, athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:
root_text.snp3.3:4.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ cīvaraṁ katvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.snp3.3:4.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso bhagavantaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.snp3.3:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.snp3.3:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.snp3.3:2.1: “Mendicants, speech that has four factors is well spoken, not poorly spoken. It’s blameless and is not criticized by sensible people.
translation_text.snp3.3:2.3: It’s when a mendicant speaks well, not poorly; their speech is principled, not unprincipled; they speak pleasantly, not unpleasantly; and they speak truthfully, not falsely.
translation_text.snp3.3:2.4: Speech with these four factors is well spoken, not poorly spoken. It’s blameless and is not criticized by sensible people.”
translation_text.snp3.3:2.5: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.snp3.3:2.6: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.snp3.3:3.1: “Good people say well-spoken words are foremost;
translation_text.snp3.3:3.2: second, speech ought be principled, not unprincipled;
translation_text.snp3.3:3.4: and fourth, speak truthfully, not falsely.”
translation_text.snp3.3:4.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.snp3.3:4.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.snp3.3:4.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.snp3.3:4.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled the Buddha in his presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.snp3.3:5.4: such speech is truly well spoken.
translation_text.snp3.3:6.4: that bring nothing bad to others.
translation_text.snp3.3:7.3: Good people say the teaching and meaning
translation_text.snp3.3:8.1: The words spoken by the Buddha
translation_text.snp3.3:8.3: for making an end of suffering,
translation_text.snp3.3:8.4: this really is the best kind of speech.”
